channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is a polar vortex? US gets a taste of the Arctic this week
item.description: The term “polar vortex” usually refers to the gigantic circular upper air weather pattern in the Arctic region. It is a normal pattern that is stronger in the winter and keeps some of the coldest weather bottled up near the North Pole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: Legendary Mexican singer Paquita la del Barrio has passed away at 77 years old. Paquita, whose real name is Francisca Viveros Barradas, passed away at her home in Veracruz, Mexico. Her family revealed her passing in a post on Monday morning on Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Bird flu likely spreading undetected in humans: CDC
item.description: Recently released data from the CDC suggests that bird flu could be spreading undetected in cows and humans.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: William Byron speaks to NewsNation after Daytona 500 win
item.description: Byron made sure to celebrate his victory, sleeping for an hour before returning to Daytona International Speedway on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron's cause of death revealed
item.description: The cause of death of South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron has been revealed. The 24-year-old's death has been ruled a suicide by the Seongdong Police Station in Seoul, according to The Korea Times.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abbott holds briefing on severe weather preparations across the state
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott on Monday morning will hold a press conference in Austin on Texas' preparation and response ahead of winter weather set to impact the state.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schwager, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump makes special appearance at NASCAR's Daytona 500 and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration cuts hit FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: Nearly half of the FDA’s $6.9 billion budget comes from fees paid by companies the agency regulates, including drug and medical device makers, which allows the agency to hire extra scientists to swiftly review products. Eliminating those positions will not reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chilean national arrested in Texas with jamming device that disabled officers' communications
item.description: A Chilean national is facing charges in Texas for burglary and interfering with arresting officers' communications by using a radio jamming device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend, as recently hired employees who review the safety of food ingredients, medical devices and other products were fired.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Conservative influencer Ashley St. Clair claims she gave birth to Elon Musk's 13th child
item.description: Conservative influencer Ashley St. Clair claims she became pregnant with Elon Musk's 13th child and secretly gave birth to their baby five months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Promising South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead at 24
item.description: A former child star in South Korea, Kim Sae-ron, has died at the age of 24, according to reports. The actress who appeared in "Bloodhounds" on Netflix, among other projects, was found dead Sunday at her home in Seoul, South Korea, according to Korean media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, KRON4

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Cher, Lady Gaga, Miley Cyrus, Snoop Dogg: 'Saturday Night Live' is feted with a concert for the ages
item.description: By the time Cher sang “If I Could Turn Back Time,” it seemed as if time had indeed been turned back, and every single “Saturday Night Live” musical guest of 50 years had magically found their way to Radio City Music Hall. Of course, it was only a smattering.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: As Trump administration reforms the EPA, cleanups of America's most toxic sites are uncertain
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just over a mile from where Patricia Flores has lived for almost 20 years, a battery smelter plant spewed toxic elements into the environment for nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two crew members ejected from a Navy jet before it crashed Wednesday off the San Diego coast and were quickly rescued by a sportfishing boat, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4
